steps.title: Recognize the linear growth model
steps.content: The equation $0.6t + 7 = 11$ follows the structure: (starting value) + (rate) × (time) = (ending value). In any linear equation, the coefficient that multiplies the time variable is the rate of change. Here, 0.6 multiplies $t$, so 0.6 is the rate.

steps.title: Determine what 0.6 represents
steps.content: Hair grows from 7 inches to 11 inches, and $t$ is measured in months. The term $0.6t$ must yield inches of growth. For dimensional consistency: (0.6 inches/month) × ($t$ months) = inches. Therefore, 0.6 is the growth rate in inches per month. B is correct.

steps.title: Why the other choices are wrong
steps.content: A is wrong because it confuses the rate with its reciprocal — if the rate were in months per inch, it would be $\frac{1}{0.6} \approx 1.67$ months per inch, not 0.6. C is wrong because 11 is the value of $0.6t + 7$ when solved (the final hair length), not the coefficient of $t$; 11 describes an outcome, not a rate. D is wrong because multiplying 0.6 by 11 gives 6.6 inches — this misreads 11 as a number of months rather than as the target length, and the result has no meaning in the problem.
